steps.dialogue: Welcome back! Today, we'll keep building on our fraction work.

Take a look at this number line with me. See how we've marked where fourths land? Last time, we focused on how to place fractions between 0 and 1, making sure each part was exactly the same size. Here, four out of four equal parts brings us to 1.

Today, we'll explore what happens when we keep counting fourths past one whole. Where would [fraction numerator=5 denominator=4]five fourths[/fraction] go? [fraction numerator=6 denominator=4]Six fourths[/fraction]?

You've worked hard at understanding a key idea: That fractions follow patterns. Your experience in the last lesson - counting each jump - is the skill you'll need today. Let's see what happens when fractions go beyond one!
